Ziba deceived Mephibosheth. Important people should always be wary of flatterers, and careful to hear both sides.

David endured Shimei’s curses much better than Ziba's flatteries; by these he was led to make a wrong judgment about another, by those to make a right judgment about himself: the world’s smiles are more dangerous than its frowns. David spared Saul’s life on multiple occasions, while Saul sought his. But innocence is no protection against malice and falsehood; nor should we be surprised if we are accused of something we have been most careful to avoid. It is good that people are not our judges, but He whose judgment is according to truth. See how patient David was under this abuse. Let this remind us of Christ, who prayed for those who reviled and crucified him. A humble spirit will turn reproaches into corrections, and gain benefit from them, instead of being provoked by them. David saw the hand of God in it, and comforted himself that God would bring good out of his affliction. We may depend upon God to repay not only our services but also our sufferings.

The wisest counselors of that age were Ahithophel and Hushai: Absalom thought himself sure of success when he had both; on them he relied, and consulted not the ark, even though he had it with him. But they were both miserable counselors. Hushai would never counsel him to act wisely. Ahithophel counseled him to do wickedly; and did as effectively betray him as he who was intentionally false to him: for those who advise people to sin certainly advise them to their harm. Ultimately, honesty is the best policy, and it will be found so in the long run. Ahithophel gave wicked counsel to Absalom, to make himself so hateful to his father that he would never be reconciled to him; this cursed policy was of the devil. How desperately wicked is the human heart!
